insight: The 'Elyot Animus' opening (DD/A as P1 in base set) is considered a cheesy aggressive strategy that is best when highly aggressive units like Grimbotch or Vores are available. In base set only (BSO), it can be countered by 'Fastimus' (fast Animus as P2) or a natural Conduit opening, with community members debating which counter is stronger.
author: liadahlia, masn6811
context: New player asking how to counter the Elyot Animus opening in base set games, prompting discussion among experienced players.

insight: In base set only (BSO), P2 can answer P1's DD/A (Elyot Animus) aggressive opening by going 'Fastimus' (natural fast Animus), since P2 has a half-turn economic lead and can afford Rhinos and Engineers more easily, and can apply maximum pressure with double Rhino on turn 4 while P1 cannot.
author: swapgo, liadahlia
context: Discussion of how P2 should respond to the Elyot Animus rush in base set games.

insight: 'Elyot Animus' refers to the P1 opening of buying two Drones then an Animus (DD/A) on turn 1-2, sacrificing economy for early aggression. 'Fastimus' refers to P2's natural fast Animus build in response. These are recognized named openings in the Prismata community for base set play.
author: liadahlia
context: Community members explaining named openings to a new player asking about base set strategy.

insight: To lose intentionally against the Pacifist Bot (which deals no attack), a player can use Vivid Drone (converts Drones to reduce supply) combined with Cluster Bolt (which constructs attack for the opponent), allowing the opponent's attack to kill you. Converting 6 Drones into 3 Vivid Drones and then constructing 10 Cluster Bolts in one turn via a Conduit is a cited method.
author: shadourow, f300xen
context: A new player asking how to intentionally lose to the Pacifist Bot for an achievement, and experienced players explaining the mechanics.

insight: Casual queue bots sometimes exhibit highly predictable mono-strategy behavior (e.g., opening DD/DD/CCC into Venge Cannon spam), making them easy to read and counter once the pattern is recognized. Master Bot is noted as appearing in casual queues in disguise, identifiable by longer think times.
author: wontrice, swapgo, supergreyman
context: New players discussing their experiences against casual queue bots and noticing predictable one-track strategies.

insight: Community consensus is that spending shards directly in the Armory on Prismatic skins is more reliable than gambling in the Black Lab, unless the player values lower-tier skins as well. The Black Lab has negative expected value for players who only want high-tier Prismatic skins.
author: awaclus, liadahlia, supergreyman
context: New player asking whether to save shards or spend them in the Black Lab cosmetic minigame.
